style_files.reason: Only semantic token authority. Values may be professionally retuned, but the namespace and ownership remain unique.
style_files.target: One restrained semantic palette, spacing, radius, motion, focus and elevation system.

style_files.reason: Canonical global reset and document layer; current content is minimal and not the main visual problem.
style_files.target: Global reset, document defaults, text rendering, safe overflow and no component styling.

style_files.reason: Owns cross-route focus and reduced-motion behavior. Preserve semantics and remove only obsolete selectors.
style_files.target: Visible focus, reduced motion, screen-reader utilities and no route-private visual decoration.

style_files.reason: Only shared component visual layer, but it is incomplete and contains unused Card styling.
style_files.target: Complete shared Button, Field, Status, Badge, Count, Notice, Empty, Error, Loading, Disclosure, Header and Dialog vocabulary.

style_files.reason: Login uses the correct functional flow but the current framed two-panel card presentation is visually generic.
style_files.target: Direct, calm login surface using shared primitives and minimal explanatory chrome.

style_files.reason: Canonical AppShell must remain, but current navigation and session presentation are visually flat and still contain compatibility hiding for deleted Workspace header/scope expressions.
style_files.target: One compact application frame, clear active navigation, restrained account/scope area and no compatibility selector residue.

style_files.reason: Primary visual debt concentration: card-heavy layout, pill-heavy status, dead selectors, selector-to-JSX mismatches and missing styles for active Evidence/Conversation class names.
style_files.target: Continuous dominant case surface, compact queue, contextual action rail, truthful Case Spine, reduced borders and clear blocker/next-action hierarchy.

style_files.reason: Patch layer exists only to override the main Workspace stylesheet and would preserve a second visual correction authority.
style_files.target: Move every still-valid rule into operator-workspace.css or shared primitives, then delete this file and import.

style_files.reason: Channels, Runtime and Control Tower share it, but the current grammar is repeated bordered panels, fact cards and KPI cards.
style_files.target: Shared administration layout primitives based on task lists, tables, sections and progressive disclosure rather than generic card grids.

style_files.reason: The three-column information architecture is useful, but every region is presented as another bordered rounded panel.
style_files.target: Library/editor/verification workspace with continuous surfaces, stronger selection, quieter metadata and a collapsible verification rail.

style_files.reason: Minified feature CSS with another filter card, run-card list, fact-card grid and JSON block vocabulary.
style_files.target: Readable source CSS using shared controls, continuous audit list/detail structure and bounded technical data presentation.

shared_components.target: Precise action hierarchy with calm hover/pressed feedback and consistent loading treatment.

replace: raw anchor navigation where router-aware navigation is required
shared_components.target: Same visual vocabulary as Button with correct link behavior.

replace: using one pill for status, source, priority, owner, count and refresh
shared_components.target: Badge is metadata only; operational state and counts receive separate shared expressions.
Consistent field density, focus, validation and help hierarchy.
Shared directional empty state that explains the next valid action.

replace: assertive live region for every error regardless of context
shared_components.target: Shared bounded error notice with clear recovery action.
One shared technical disclosure presentation across Workspace, Channels and Runtime.
Compact, high-confidence confirmation surface using the same action hierarchy.

replace: eyebrow as a default page pattern
shared_components.target: One consistent task header with optional context, not marketing-page decoration.

missing_shared_responsibilities.name: StatusIndicator
missing_shared_responsibilities.reason: Operational state is currently forced through Badge pills.
missing_shared_responsibilities.rule: Must become the only shared operational-state visual expression and migrate relevant Badge consumers.

missing_shared_responsibilities.name: Count
missing_shared_responsibilities.reason: Counts and totals are currently rendered as status pills.
missing_shared_responsibilities.rule: Pure numeric presentation using tabular figures; no semantic color unless the count itself represents risk.

missing_shared_responsibilities.name: Notice
missing_shared_responsibilities.reason: Warning, information, degraded and success receipts are implemented through unrelated feature classes.
missing_shared_responsibilities.rule: One shared semantic notice component; feature-private alert boxes migrate and are deleted.

missing_shared_responsibilities.name: LoadingState
missing_shared_responsibilities.reason: Loading currently reuses EmptyState or plain text and does not consistently preserve page structure.
missing_shared_responsibilities.rule: Use structural skeletons where shape is known and bounded status text where it is not.

systemic_findings.evidence: Badge is used for priority, source, owner, SLA, refresh, account counts, task totals, runtime state and action outcomes.
systemic_findings.root_fix: Separate metadata Badge, StatusIndicator and Count responsibilities.

systemic_findings.finding: Card and border grammar is the default structure
systemic_findings.evidence: Workspace, Knowledge, Control Tower, Channels, Runtime and Runtime Audit repeatedly use surface + border + radius containers and nested fact/KPI cards.
systemic_findings.root_fix: Replace nested cards with continuous surfaces, sections, rows, dividers and task-based grouping.

systemic_findings.finding: Active shared states lack a complete visual implementation
systemic_findings.evidence: EmptyState and ErrorSummary output shared classes without corresponding shared CSS definitions; TechnicalDetails has only partial route-specific styling.
systemic_findings.root_fix: Complete shared state and disclosure styling before route redesign.

systemic_findings.evidence: JSX renders operator-evidence-panel, operator-evidence-list and operator-conversation-panel while the main stylesheet styles older operator-evidence and operator-conversation selectors. CSS also retains operator-app-header and operator-scope although the canonical AppShell owns them.
systemic_findings.root_fix: Rebuild the Workspace stylesheet against current JSX, then delete dead selectors and the refinement patch layer.

systemic_findings.finding: Case journey lacks a dominant visual structure
systemic_findings.evidence: The approved Case Spine is not visibly implemented; hierarchy is carried by headings, explanatory copy, panels and pills instead.
systemic_findings.root_fix: Introduce a truthful Case Spine only from durable/server-provided facts and make blocker plus next action dominant.

systemic_findings.evidence: operationalPresentation.ts, supportStatus.ts and operatorWorkspacePresentation.ts all project labels and tones for different routes.
systemic_findings.root_fix: Preserve domain-specific mapping functions but standardize their visual output contract through shared StatusIndicator/Badge/Notice primitives.

systemic_findings.finding: Operator-visible names are inconsistent in specificity
systemic_findings.evidence: Labels range from generic Workbench/Knowledge/Overview wording to internal Tenant, Ticket, Job and server-authority explanations.
systemic_findings.root_fix: Create one visible terminology map and move implementation identifiers behind technical disclosure.

systemic_findings.finding: Runtime audit CSS is minified and visually independent
systemic_findings.evidence: runtime-evidence-audit.css is a single minified line and recreates filters, cards, facts and list-selection styling.
systemic_findings.root_fix: Rewrite as readable CSS consuming shared layout and component vocabulary.

route_dispositions.visual_target: Direct operator login without generic marketing/card storytelling.

route_dispositions.preserve: Queue, selection, deep links, drafts, actions, authorization, confirmations and refresh behavior
route_dispositions.visual_target: Dense calm logistics cockpit with dominant case surface, compact queue, Case Spine, blocker and one next action.

route_dispositions.preserve: Search, selection, draft guard, edit, publish and retrieval test
route_dispositions.visual_target: Knowledge library/editor with a quieter verification rail and less panel chrome.

route_dispositions.preserve: Account health, onboarding task lifecycle and confirmation behavior
route_dispositions.visual_target: Channel operations surface with health and work queues, not form-card grids.

route_dispositions.preserve: Runtime reads and evidence audit behavior
route_dispositions.visual_target: Technical operations surface with clear ready/degraded/failed hierarchy and progressive detail.
Actionable risk and workload list; remove generic KPI-card dashboard grammar.

external_framework_decision.principle: An external framework may be selected only as a full replacement authority with same-path migration and deletion, or used as a design/code donor. It may not coexist as a second visual system.
external_framework_decision.current_preference: Keep React and Radix behavior; evaluate external visual recipes after the inventory, before implementation.

acceptance: Every active source CSS file is listed in this inventory.
Every style file has exactly one disposition.
Every new shared primitive replaces an identified missing responsibility.
FOLD_AND_DELETE and MIGRATE_AND_DELETE items are physically removed before acceptance.
No visual rewrite changes backend, authorization or business truth.
No old/new UI switch or parallel framework remains.
